title: Uniform & Stationery
metaDescription: Find Rosehill College uniform and stationery information for Years 9 to 13. Stationery can be purchased online, and uniforms are available at The Uniform Shoppe Papakura.

sections.heading: Uniform and stationery
sections.lede: Uniform and stationery information for Years 9 to 13. Stationery can be purchased online, and uniform is available from The Uniform Shoppe in Papakura.

sections.heading: Rosehill College uniform
sections.lede: Students are not able to wear their sports hoodies or sports uniform to school — these can only be worn when participating in their codes and practices.
sections.caption: Rosehill College uniform requirements
rows:
Unisex navy long pants and/or shorts, or a navy skirt (short and long are available).
No tartan skirts. Please note: no black pants or shorts to be worn.
Blue shirts with embroidered sleeves and school crest. Available in a men's and a women's cut.

White shirts with embroidered sleeves and school crest. Available in a men's and a women's cut.
Blazer and school tie are optional, except for student leaders.
Worn for all House activities, fun days and sports events within school, and as the Physical Education uniform for practical classes.
All-black lace-up shoes, black Roman sandals or black Burgan stocks.
sections.note: All new students to Rosehill College will purchase the new uniform.

images.caption: Years 9 to 11 — blue shirts with embroidered sleeves and school crest
Years 12 and 13 — white shirts, with optional blazer and tie

images.alt: Rosehill College House shirts worn for Physical Education
images.caption: House shirts — compulsory, worn for House activities and Physical Education

sections.heading: Uniform shop

image.alt: The uniform shop in Papakura that stocks the Rosehill College uniform

sections.html:
Rosehill College uniform can be purchased from SAS, or ordered online from The Uniform Shoppe.

28a Wood Street, Papakura
Phone 09 296 6063

Shop hours
Monday – Thursday: 8.30am – 5.00pm
Saturday: 9.00am – 1.00pm
Friday & Sunday: closed

sections.heading: Stationery lists 2026
sections.lede: Stationery is not sold by the school. You are welcome to purchase stationery from OfficeMax or any outside supplier such as Warehouse Stationery or a local high street chain.

sections.heading: Ready for the first day?
sections.text: Start-of-school information, term dates and the student handbook are all ready for whānau.
